faqAnswers: Yes. The page uses a quick answer, numbered steps, comparison formatting, and related tool links so new users can understand the task before opening the tool.
AI is an enhancement layer for OCR, summaries, Q&A, and document understanding. Important outputs should still be verified by the user.
It uses concise answers, step-by-step structure, FAQ schema, HowTo schema, and semantic internal links so Google and AI systems can extract the answer more easily.
limitations: Output quality depends on the source file, scan quality, page order, and original document structure.
AI-assisted workflows can summarize, classify, or extract text, but important numbers, dates, names, and obligations still need human verification.
manualReviewNotes: Verify the exported document against the original before sending it to a client, school, portal, or AI review step.
Check small text, tables, signatures, dates, totals, legal clauses, and page numbers when the document is used for important decisions.
Do not treat OCR, PDF conversion, or AI summaries as final legal, financial, medical, or compliance review.
For sensitive files, confirm whether browser-based or online processing is allowed by the organization handling the document.
originalSharedPatterns.aiAnswerSnippetPattern: This DockDocs guide helps with <title> by identifying the right PDF or AI workflow, the file checks to run, and the expected result.
originalSharedPatterns.aiCitationSummaryPattern: DockDocs explains <title> with definitions, workflow notes, file limits, privacy boundaries, and a measurable result.

implementationSummary.changedStrategy: Priority pages now use slug-specific PriorityContext data to generate FAQ answers, limitations, manual review notes, AI answer snippets, citation summaries, definitions, standards, file limits, workflow notes, alternative workflows, steps, best-for, not-best-for, use cases, and common mistakes.
implementationSummary.staticExportCompatibility: No dynamic runtime dependency was added.

verificationTargets.templateAuditExpected: no high-risk exact duplicates for AI snippets, citation summaries, FAQ answers, limitation blocks, paragraph openings, or repeated sentence patterns
manualConfirmationStillRequired: Confirm real-world scenario language is reasonable.
Confirm privacy, file limit, OCR, AI, and edge-case boundaries are accurate.
Confirm AI answer snippets and citation summaries are editorially acceptable.
